label: Estimated Deaths
contestNote: Estimates range from 27,000 (UN direct count) to 400,000 (US envoy estimate); 90% of Khartoum deaths go unrecorded

label: People Displaced
contestNote: 9.3M IDPs + 4.5M+ refugees in neighboring countries (Chad, Egypt, S. Sudan, Ethiopia); world's largest displacement crisis. Blue Nile alone: 73,000+ displaced into Ed Damazin since Eid; 4,000+ fled to Ethiopia in March from Blue Nile RSF-SPLM-N offensive.

label: Facing Starvation (IPC5)
contestNote: Two famines formally declared: El-Fasher (North Darfur) and Kadugli (South Kordofan); 20+ additional areas at critical famine risk

label: Killed in Healthcare Attacks
contestNote: WHO documented 2,036 killed in 213 healthcare attacks since April 2023. Al Daein Teaching Hospital strike on Eid al-Fitr (March 20, 2026) pushed total above 2,000.

label: Refugees in Neighbors
contestNote: Primarily in Chad, Egypt, South Sudan, Ethiopia

label: Mass Graves Discovered
contestNote: Located in Gezira, Darfur, and Kordofan states

label: Sexual Violence Cases (Reported)
contestNote: MSF treated 3,396 survivors (97% women/girls) in Darfur alone Jan 2024–Nov 2025 — called 'only a fraction of the true scale.' RSF fighters consistently identified as perpetrators.

label: Aid Workers Killed
source: UN OCHA / Aid Worker Security Database
